target.name: Hailo
target.founder: Orr Danon, Hadar Zeitlin, Avi Baum

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $336M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Hailo is a Series D semiconductor design company that has raised $336M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip competitors, with particular focus on 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers: SambaNova Systems ($5.0B Series D), Tenstorrent ($2.7B Series D), and DEEPX ($529M Series C).

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $336M total raised as a proxy for recent valuation, applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yielding $504M. Peer-median analysis across the three private comparables produces a $2.7B median valuation, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (24.2x median across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of Hailo's disclosed revenue.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $428M (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The wide spread reflects material dispersion among peer valuations at this frontier-chip stage. Key uncertainties include Hailo's product-market traction relative to better-capitalized peers like SambaNova, and whether recent private capital deployment has compressed or widened valuation gaps within the cohort. Revenue visibility and path to profitability remain critical discriminators not resolved by comparable analysis alone.
